text.source_text: अपि चे दृग्रूपत्वं प्राज्ञस्यैवात्मनः स्मर्यते भगवद्गीतासु — ‘ न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः । यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ’ (भ. गी. १५ । ६) इति । ‘ यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भासयतेऽखिलम् । यच्चन्द्रमसि यच्चाग्नौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम् ’ (भ. गी. १५ । १२) इति च ॥ २३ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: api ce dṛgrūpatvaṃ prājñasyaivātmanaḥ smaryate bhagavadgītāsu — ‘ na tadbhāsayate sūryo na śaśāṅko na pāvakaḥ | yadgatvā na nivartante taddhāma paramaṃ mama ’ (bha. gī. 15 | 6) iti | ‘ yadādityagataṃ tejo jagadbhāsayate'khilam | yaccandramasi yaccāgnau tattejo viddhi māmakam ’ (bha. gī. 15 | 12) iti ca || 23 ||

translation.literal_translation: And this light-as-seer nature of the conscious Self is also remembered in the Bhagavad Gita: 'That is not illumined by sun, moon, or fire; having gone there, they do not return - that is my highest abode,' and 'the splendor in sun, moon, and fire, know that splendor to be mine.'
translation.technical_translation: Sutra 1.3.23 supporting smriti-pramana: Gita passages corroborate Brahman as source-light beyond derivative celestial luminosity.
translation.interpretive_note: Smriti corroboration aligns with the upanishadic jyotir reading.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

A later philological sweep should revisit light-metaphysics language for strict terminological consistency.
